findings.title: Insufficient Granular Consent Mechanisms
findings.description: Current consent mechanisms are perceived as insufficient, particularly for cross-device tracking and data sharing with advertisers, which may lead to legal compliance issues.
assumptions: Users require more control over their data tracking preferences.

evidence.excerpt: Users require more granular consent options for data tracking, especially regarding cross-device tracking and data sharing with advertisers.

recommendations: Develop granular consent options for users to manage their data tracking preferences.

metadata.mitigation: Implement granular consent mechanisms for user data tracking.

findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit Blocker
findings.description: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch, which is a critical blocker for the EU market. Key concerns include lawful basis for processing, consent mechanisms, data retention policies, right to erasure, and completion of the DPIA.
assumptions: Compliance with GDPR is essential for operating in the EU market.

evidence.excerpt: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch.

recommendations: Prepare for a full GDPR compliance audit by addressing key concerns.

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit.

findings.title: Privacy Risks in User Data Handling
findings.description: There are significant privacy risks associated with the handling of user data, particularly concerning the potential for re-identification in the data export for advertisers and cross-device tracking.
assumptions: User data handling practices need to minimize re-identification risks.

evidence.excerpt: There are significant privacy risks associated with the handling of user data, particularly concerning the potential for re-identification.

recommendations: Implement measures to minimize re-identification risks in data handling.

metadata.mitigation: Enhance data handling practices to reduce re-identification risks.

findings.title: Authentication Weaknesses Detected
findings.description: There are potential weaknesses in the authentication process that could lead to unauthorized access to user data.
assumptions: Authentication mechanisms need to be robust to prevent unauthorized access.

evidence.excerpt: Detected auth risk keywords in 3 source(s).

recommendations: Strengthen authentication mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access.

metadata.mitigation: Enhance authentication processes to ensure security.

findings.title: Compliance Risks Related to Data Handling
findings.description: The handling of user data may not comply with GDPR and CCPA regulations, particularly regarding lawful processing and consent.
assumptions: Compliance with data protection regulations is critical for the product.

evidence.excerpt: Detected compliance risk keywords in 5 source(s).

recommendations: Review data handling practices to ensure compliance with GDPR and CCPA.

metadata.mitigation: Conduct a compliance review of data handling practices.

summary: Identified 5 risks (2 compliance, 2 privacy, 1 security). 3 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (4 blockers, 2 warnings).
